analysis.core_story_summary: [From the Pratisarga Parva, Chapters Four to Seven. ] Suta Goswami said: Once upon a time in Hastinapura, Pradyota the son of Kshemaka was leading an assembly and meanwhile the great sage Narada arrived there. King Pradyota happily honored him.

audience_notes.children_8_12: Use simple language, explain divine names as characters in a sacred legend.
audience_notes.teens_13_17: Connect events to decision-making, identity, and consequences.
audience_notes.young_adults_18_30: Highlight ethics, leadership, and social relevance.
audience_notes.devotional_traditional: Preserve sacred framing and theological terms respectfully.
audience_notes.global_general: Keep cultural context brief and avoid insider assumptions.

sensitivity_notes: Contains conflict imagery that may need age-appropriate framing.
Includes intense mythic elements (curses, demons, destruction).
References family and marriage roles rooted in traditional contexts.

redesigned_story_by_demographic.children_8_12: **BIBLICAL AND MODERN HISTORY — Kids (8-12):** [From the Pratisarga Parva, Chapters Four to Seven. ] Suta Goswami said: Once upon a time in Hastinapura, Pradyota the son of Kshemaka was leading an assembly and meanwhile the great sage Narada arrived there. King Pradyota happily honored him. This story teaches devotion and disciplined practice, righteous duty, divine grace, inner focus and self-mastery. Think of it like a legend that helps us choose kindness, courage, and respect.
redesigned_story_by_demographic.teens_13_17: **BIBLICAL AND MODERN HISTORY — Teens (13-17):** [From the Pratisarga Parva, Chapters Four to Seven. ] Suta Goswami said: Once upon a time in Hastinapura, Pradyota the son of Kshemaka was leading an assembly and meanwhile the great sage Narada arrived there. King Pradyota happily honored him. For teens, the key idea is devotion and disciplined practice, righteous duty, divine grace, inner focus and self-mastery. It shows how choices, discipline, and character shape outcomes.
redesigned_story_by_demographic.young_adults_18_30: **BIBLICAL AND MODERN HISTORY — Young Adults:** [From the Pratisarga Parva, Chapters Four to Seven. ] Suta Goswami said: Once upon a time in Hastinapura, Pradyota the son of Kshemaka was leading an assembly and meanwhile the great sage Narada arrived there. King Pradyota happily honored him. A modern takeaway is devotion and disciplined practice, righteous duty, divine grace, inner focus and self-mastery. The narrative can be read as a reflection on purpose, responsibility, and values-driven action.
redesigned_story_by_demographic.devotional_traditional: **BIBLICAL AND MODERN HISTORY — Devotional/Traditional Readers:** [From the Pratisarga Parva, Chapters Four to Seven. ] Suta Goswami said: Once upon a time in Hastinapura, Pradyota the son of Kshemaka was leading an assembly and meanwhile the great sage Narada arrived there. King Pradyota happily honored him. The episode highlights devotion and disciplined practice, righteous duty, divine grace, inner focus and self-mastery. It preserves reverence for divine agency while encouraging faithful practice and humility.
redesigned_story_by_demographic.global_general: **BIBLICAL AND MODERN HISTORY — Global General Audience:** [From the Pratisarga Parva, Chapters Four to Seven. ] Suta Goswami said: Once upon a time in Hastinapura, Pradyota the son of Kshemaka was leading an assembly and meanwhile the great sage Narada arrived there. King Pradyota happily honored him. In universal terms, it explores devotion and disciplined practice, righteous duty, divine grace, inner focus and self-mastery.
